title: Zelkova davidii
taxonomy: Zelkova davidii belongs to the family Ulmaceae, which is a group of deciduous trees commonly known as elm or elm family. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Zelkova. Zelkova davidii is native to China and is closely related to other Zelkova species, which are known for their vase-shaped crowns and deciduous leaves. The genus Zelkova is further classified under the order Rosales, which includes several families of flowering plants. This classification places Zelkova davidii within the broader context of flowering plants in the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Zelkova davidii is known to have various medicinal properties due to its rich source of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have also been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In traditional medicine, Zelkova davidii is used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully explore the potential medicinal applications of this plant.

intro: Zelkova davidii, also known as David's zelkova, is a deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of China and Tibet, belonging to the family Ulmaceae. The bark and roots of Zelkova davidii have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Zelkova davidii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Zelkova davidii is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal formulas to enhance their therapeutic effects.
